80322064.title: Restore Britain

pageprops.wikibase-shortdesc: Political party in the United Kingdom

80322064.description: Political party in the United Kingdom
80322064.extract:
Restore Britain is a political party in the United Kingdom led by Rupert Lowe, the Member of Parliament for Great Yarmouth. The organisation was launched on 30 June 2025 as a political movement and pressure group, and was announced as a political party on 13 February 2026. It presents itself as an umbrella organisation for local grassroots organisations. Various journalists and commentators have described Restore Britain as right-wing or far-right in the context of British party politics.
Lowe left Reform UK after a public dispute with the party's leadership, including criticism of Nigel Farage. Reform UK subsequently alleged threatening behaviour, which Lowe and his staff denied. Later in 2025 he established Restore Britain as a pressure group, with an initial advisory board that initially included Conservative politicians such as Susan Hall and Gavin Williamson.
A central early activity of Restore Britain was fundraising for an enquiry into the grooming gangs, a sexual abuse scandal within the United Kingdom. After Farage announced but did not proceed with an independent enquiry under Reform UK, Lowe raised several hundred thousand pounds via crowdfunding to support a separate investigation associated with Restore Britain.
In February 2026, Lowe stated that Restore Britain would transition from a pressure group into a national political party. He has argued for "full‑scale restoration" rather than incremental reform and has presented the party as a break from existing political leadership. Restore Britain advocates policies such as large-scale deportation of people in the United Kingdom without legal status, seeking net-negative immigration, reinstating the death penalty, reducing certain taxes and the size of the state, withdrawing public funding for the BBC, banning the burqa and niqab, legalising the possession of pepper spray and expanding the legal scope of "reasonable force" in defence of the home. The party has also referred to stopping wokery and abolishing kosher and halal slaughter in an effort to restore Christian principles.
